shortDescription: A bright red pixel shrimp with black-rimmed glasses typing on a tiny keyboard.
longDescription: Shrimpy Keys is a compact pixel-art coding companion: a bright red shrimp in black-rimmed glasses, typing away on a tiny keyboard. The pet includes standard Codex animation states and v2 look-direction support for all 16 gaze directions.
publicationSubtitle: A tiny shrimp coder with glasses, a keyboard, and very serious typing energy.
samplePrompts: Use Shrimpy Keys as a cheerful coding companion for a developer workspace.
Show Shrimpy Keys waiting for approval with an expectant keyboard-ready pose.
Use Shrimpy Keys as a playful mascot for debugging, review, and focused build sessions.

listingCopy.oneLine: A pixel shrimp coder with glasses, a tiny keyboard, and 16-direction v2 gaze support.
listingCopy.short: Shrimpy Keys brings tiny crustacean coding energy to Codex, complete with glasses, keyboard animations, and v2 look directions.
listingCopy.long: Shrimpy Keys is a bright red pixel shrimp built for developer companionship. It types, waits, waves, reviews, reacts to failures, and looks around using the Codex v2 16-direction gaze rows while keeping its chunky glasses and tiny keyboard identity intact.
